---
title: "Fasting, Body Composition, and Lifestyle Patterns by Age Group"
format: html
---

## Analysis Objective

This report explores how fasting measurements, BMI, body size, sex, and lifestyle-related variables vary across two age groups:

- `Age <= 30`
- `Age > 30`

The objective is to identify descriptive patterns, compare distributions, and examine how anthropometric and metabolic variables behave across these two age-based segments.

## Import necessary libraries

```{python}
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
from dotenv import load_dotenv
```

## Set the visual theme

```{python}
sns.set_theme(
    style="whitegrid",
    context="notebook",
    rc={"figure.figsize": (8, 5)}
)
```

## Load the dataset

```{python}
load_dotenv()

dataset_path = os.getenv("CLEANED_DATASET_PATH")
if dataset_path is None:
    raise ValueError("CLEANED_DATASET_PATH is not set in the environment.")

df = pd.read_excel(dataset_path)
df.head()
```

## Initial data overview

### Shape of the dataset

```{python}
df.shape
```

### Column names

```{python}
df.columns.tolist()
```

### Check required columns

```{python}
required_columns = [
    "Age",
    "Sex",
    "Height (CM)",
    "Weight (KG)",
    "BMI",
    "Fasting"
]

missing_columns = [col for col in required_columns if col not in df.columns]

if missing_columns:
    raise ValueError(f"Missing required columns: {missing_columns}")
else:
    print("All required columns are present.")
```

### Data types

```{python}
df.dtypes
```

### Missing values summary

```{python}
df[required_columns].isnull().sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
```

### Duplicate rows

```{python}
df.duplicated().sum()
```

## Descriptive statistics

### Numerical summary

```{python}
df[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]].describe()
```

### Categorical summary

```{python}
categorical_columns = ["Sex"] + (["Diet"] if "Diet" in df.columns else [])

for col in categorical_columns:
    print(f"\nValue counts for: {col}")
    print(df[col].value_counts(dropna=False))
```

## Data cleaning and outlier handling

The goal here is to remove implausible or extreme records that may distort the analysis.

```{python}
df = df.drop_duplicates().copy()

df = df[
    ~(
        (df["BMI"] >= 60) |
        (df["BMI"] <= 15) |
        (df["Weight (KG)"] >= 500) |
        (df["Weight (KG)"] <= 30) |
        (df["Height (CM)"] > 250) |
        (df["Height (CM)"] <= 100) |
        (df["Fasting"] > 50)
    )
].copy()
```

### Quick sanity check after filtering

```{python}
df[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]].describe()
```

## Feature engineering

### Create age group labels

```{python}
df["Age Group"] = np.where(df["Age"] <= 30, "<=30", ">30")
df["Age Group"].value_counts()
```

### Create BMI category

```{python}
bmi_bins = [0, 18.5, 25, 30, np.inf]
bmi_labels = ["Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", "Obese"]

df["BMI Category"] = pd.cut(
    df["BMI"],
    bins=bmi_bins,
    labels=bmi_labels,
    right=False
)

df["BMI Category"].value_counts(dropna=False)
```

## Sample composition

### Participant count by age group

```{python}
ax = sns.countplot(data=df, x="Age Group", hue="Age Group", palette="Set2", legend=False)
ax.set(
    xlabel="Age Group",
    ylabel="Count",
    title="Participant Distribution by Age Group"
)
```

### Sex distribution across age groups

```{python}
ax = sns.countplot(data=df, x="Age Group", hue="Sex", palette="Set1")
ax.set(
    xlabel="Age Group",
    ylabel="Count",
    title="Sex Distribution Across Age Groups"
)
```

### BMI category distribution by age group

```{python}
g = sns.catplot(
    data=df,
    x="BMI Category",
    hue="Age Group",
    kind="count",
    palette="Set2",
    height=5,
    aspect=1.6
)

g.set_axis_labels("BMI Category", "Count")
g.set_titles("BMI Category Distribution by Age Group")
g.ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15)
```

## Grouped descriptive analytics

### Summary statistics by age group

```{python}
group_summary = (
    df.groupby("Age Group")[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]]
    .agg(["count", "mean", "median", "std", "min", "max"])
    .round(2)
)

group_summary
```

### Summary statistics by age group and sex

```{python}
group_sex_summary = (
    df.groupby(["Age Group", "Sex"])[["Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]]
    .agg(["count", "mean", "median", "std"])
    .round(2)
)

group_sex_summary
```

## Correlation analysis

### Overall correlation heatmap

```{python}
corr = df[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]].corr(numeric_only=True)

ax = sns.heatmap(
    corr,
    annot=True,
    cmap="coolwarm",
    fmt=".2f",
    linewidths=0.5
)

ax.set_title("Correlation Heatmap of Core Numerical Variables")
```

### Correlation heatmap for `Age <= 30`

```{python}
corr_young = (
    df.loc[df["Age Group"] == "<=30", ["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]]
    .corr(numeric_only=True)
)

ax = sns.heatmap(
    corr_young,
    annot=True,
    cmap="coolwarm",
    fmt=".2f",
    linewidths=0.5
)

ax.set_title("Correlation Heatmap for Age <= 30")
```

### Correlation heatmap for `Age > 30`

```{python}
corr_older = (
    df.loc[df["Age Group"] == ">30", ["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]]
    .corr(numeric_only=True)
)

ax = sns.heatmap(
    corr_older,
    annot=True,
    cmap="coolwarm",
    fmt=".2f",
    linewidths=0.5
)

ax.set_title("Correlation Heatmap for Age > 30")
```

## Distribution analysis

### Fasting distribution overall

```{python}
ax = sns.histplot(
    data=df,
    x="Fasting",
    kde=True,
    bins=30
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Fasting",
    ylabel="Count",
    title="Overall Distribution of Fasting"
)
```

### Fasting distribution by age group

```{python}
ax = sns.histplot(
    data=df,
    x="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    kde=True,
    bins=30,
    element="step",
    stat="count",
    common_norm=False
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Fasting",
    ylabel="Count",
    title="Fasting Distribution by Age Group"
)
```

### BMI distribution overall

```{python}
ax = sns.histplot(
    data=df,
    x="BMI",
    kde=True,
    bins=30
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="BMI",
    ylabel="Count",
    title="Overall Distribution of BMI"
)
```

### BMI distribution by age group

```{python}
ax = sns.histplot(
    data=df,
    x="BMI",
    hue="Age Group",
    kde=True,
    bins=30,
    element="step",
    stat="count",
    common_norm=False
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="BMI",
    ylabel="Count",
    title="BMI Distribution by Age Group"
)
```

### Density comparison of fasting by age group

```{python}
ax = sns.kdeplot(
    data=df,
    x="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    fill=True,
    common_norm=False,
    alpha=0.4
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Fasting",
    ylabel="Density",
    title="Density of Fasting by Age Group"
)
```

## Core relationship analysis

### Age vs fasting

#### Scatter plot

```{python}
ax = sns.scatterplot(
    data=df,
    x="Age",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    palette="Set1",
    alpha=0.7
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Age",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="Age vs Fasting"
)
```

#### Mean fasting by age

```{python}
age_fasting = (
    df.groupby(["Age", "Age Group"], as_index=False)["Fasting"]
    .mean()
)

ax = sns.lineplot(
    data=age_fasting,
    x="Age",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    marker="o"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Age",
    ylabel="Mean Fasting",
    title="Mean Fasting by Age"
)
```

### BMI vs fasting

#### Scatter plot by age group

```{python}
ax = sns.scatterplot(
    data=df,
    x="BMI",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    alpha=0.7,
    palette="Set2"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="BMI",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="BMI vs Fasting by Age Group"
)
```

#### Regression view

```{python}
g = sns.lmplot(
    data=df,
    x="BMI",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    height=5,
    aspect=1.4,
    scatter_kws={"alpha": 0.6}
)

g.set_axis_labels("BMI", "Fasting")
```

### Weight vs fasting

```{python}
ax = sns.scatterplot(
    data=df,
    x="Weight (KG)",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    alpha=0.7,
    palette="Set1"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Weight (KG)",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="Weight vs Fasting by Age Group"
)
```

### Height vs fasting

```{python}
ax = sns.scatterplot(
    data=df,
    x="Height (CM)",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    alpha=0.7,
    palette="Set2"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Height (CM)",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="Height vs Fasting by Age Group"
)
```

## Categorical comparisons

### Sex vs fasting

```{python}
ax = sns.boxplot(
    data=df,
    x="Sex",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    palette="Set2"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Sex",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="Fasting by Sex and Age Group"
)
```

### Sex vs BMI

```{python}
ax = sns.boxplot(
    data=df,
    x="Sex",
    y="BMI",
    hue="Age Group",
    palette="Set1"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Sex",
    ylabel="BMI",
    title="BMI by Sex and Age Group"
)
```

### Age group vs fasting

```{python}
ax = sns.boxplot(
    data=df,
    x="Age Group",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    palette="Set2",
    legend=False
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Age Group",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="Fasting by Age Group"
)
```

### Age group vs BMI

```{python}
ax = sns.boxplot(
    data=df,
    x="Age Group",
    y="BMI",
    hue="Age Group",
    palette="Set1",
    legend=False
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Age Group",
    ylabel="BMI",
    title="BMI by Age Group"
)
```

### Violin plot of fasting by age group

```{python}
ax = sns.violinplot(
    data=df,
    x="Age Group",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Age Group",
    palette="Pastel1",
    legend=False
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Age Group",
    ylabel="Fasting",
    title="Fasting Distribution Shape by Age Group"
)
```

## Diet-related analysis

This section runs only if the `Diet` column exists.

### Diet frequency table

```{python}
if "Diet" in df.columns:
    df["Diet"].value_counts(dropna=False)
else:
    print("Diet column not found in the dataset.")
```

### Diet vs BMI

```{python}
if "Diet" in df.columns:
    g = sns.catplot(
        data=df,
        x="Diet",
        y="BMI",
        hue="Age Group",
        kind="box",
        height=5,
        aspect=1.8
    )

    g.set_axis_labels("Diet", "BMI")
    g.ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=20)
else:
    print("Diet column not found in the dataset.")
```

### Diet vs fasting

```{python}
if "Diet" in df.columns:
    g = sns.catplot(
        data=df,
        x="Diet",
        y="Fasting",
        hue="Age Group",
        kind="box",
        height=5,
        aspect=1.8
    )

    g.set_axis_labels("Diet", "Fasting")
    g.ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=20)
else:
    print("Diet column not found in the dataset.")
```

### Diet composition across age groups

```{python}
if "Diet" in df.columns:
    g = sns.catplot(
        data=df,
        x="Diet",
        hue="Age Group",
        kind="count",
        height=5,
        aspect=1.8
    )

    g.set_axis_labels("Diet", "Count")
    g.ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=20)
else:
    print("Diet column not found in the dataset.")
```

## Multivariable relationship analysis

### Age and sex to fasting

```{python}
ax = sns.lineplot(
    data=df,
    x="Age",
    y="Fasting",
    hue="Sex",
    style="Age Group",
    estimator="mean"
)

ax.set(
    xlabel="Age",
    ylabel="Mean Fasting",
    title="Mean Fasting by Age, Sex, and Age Group"
)
```

### BMI vs fasting faceted by age group

```{python}
g = sns.relplot(
    data=df,
    x="BMI",
    y="Fasting",
    col="Age Group",
    hue="Sex",
    kind="scatter",
    alpha=0.7,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.1
)

g.set_axis_labels("BMI", "Fasting")
g.set_titles("Age Group: {col_name}")
```

### Weight vs fasting faceted by age group

```{python}
g = sns.relplot(
    data=df,
    x="Weight (KG)",
    y="Fasting",
    col="Age Group",
    hue="Sex",
    kind="scatter",
    alpha=0.7,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.1
)

g.set_axis_labels("Weight (KG)", "Fasting")
g.set_titles("Age Group: {col_name}")
```

### Pairwise view of core variables

```{python}
pairplot_df = df[
    ["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting", "Age Group"]
].dropna()

sns.pairplot(
    pairplot_df,
    hue="Age Group",
    corner=True,
    diag_kind="hist"
)
```

## Group comparison tables

### Mean comparison across age groups

```{python}
mean_comparison = (
    df.groupby("Age Group")[["Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]]
    .mean()
    .round(2)
    .sort_index()
)

mean_comparison
```

### Median comparison across age groups

```{python}
median_comparison = (
    df.groupby("Age Group")[["Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]]
    .median()
    .round(2)
    .sort_index()
)

median_comparison
```

### Sex-wise mean fasting within each age group

```{python}
sex_age_fasting = (
    df.groupby(["Age Group", "Sex"])["Fasting"]
    .mean()
    .round(2)
    .unstack()
)

sex_age_fasting
```

### BMI category by age group

```{python}
bmi_age_table = pd.crosstab(df["BMI Category"], df["Age Group"])
bmi_age_table
```
